rationale: Risk.net Cutting Edge piece on real-time telemetry/authorisation for bank agentic AI hits the SR 11-7/PRA SS1/23 governance pain directly, giving FS senior leaders a concrete runtime-control framework to evaluate for agentic deployments.

BoE Bank Underground piece on agentic commerce payment infrastructure signals regulatory framing is forming around AI agents executing transactions — a direct priority shift for payments, compliance, and senior FS leaders.

Practical agentic sandbox solving real isolation pain (GPU passthrough, sudo, browser-use, persistent env) for engineers running autonomous coding agents on headless Linux — directly adoptable this week.

OSCAR delivers precomputed INT2 KV-cache rotation matrices on HuggingFace with an arXiv paper, giving local-inference engineers a concrete path to 2-bit KV quantization that cuts memory without retraining.

New benchmark exposing that LLM-based GPU kernel agents overfit sandboxes and fail in production inference stacks — directly relevant to engineers building or evaluating agentic code-gen for inference optimization.

llama.cpp PR fixes KV-cache checkpoint bug that forces full context reprocessing in agentic coding workflows (opencode etc.), directly unblocking local-model users running long agentic sessions today.

OpenSkillEval offers a concrete eval framework for auditing LLM agent skills/workflows—directly useful for engineers selecting or building skills for agent pipelines, but no FS-specific angle and no code/repo confirmed yet.

New arXiv finding that VLMs barely degrade when image tokens are removed challenges benchmark validity—directly relevant to any DS evaluating or deploying multimodal models, but no code/repo yet and limited FS-specific angle.

EU AI Act compliance guidance for finance functions hits senior leaders and model-risk teams squarely, but single low-trust source with no artefacts limits utility; regulatory framing is real but execution detail unclear.

Entropy-phase-transition framing for when CoT helps vs. hurts is a practical signal for engineers deciding inference strategy and token budgets, but no code/benchmark released yet.

KG-R1 uses RL to collapse multi-module KG-RAG pipelines into a single agentic framework, cutting inference cost and schema lock-in — directly relevant to FS teams using KGs for fraud/AML or verifiable reasoning, but no public repo yet limits immediate adoption.
ProxyCoT addresses a real gap—LLMs failing on long-context reasoning despite 10M-token windows—with a training framework, but no public code/weights yet limits immediate adoption.

Claim-level RAG eval benchmark for legal text is directly relevant to FS teams building RAG over contracts/regulations, but paper-only with no code/repo confirmed and narrow domain limits broader utility.
